prompt:
A neatly arranged tracker frame kit laid out on a workshop surface, including pre-cut aluminum extrusions, corner brackets, pivot hardware, and stainless fasteners sized for a compact 200 Wh solar tracker build. The pieces should be organized as a ready-to-assemble kit, with distinct structural components visible and no enclosure dominating the scene. The layout should clearly communicate modular frame hardware rather than a finished solar device.

Ultra-detailed, photorealistic 512×512 image with a grounded near-future maker-energy aesthetic: soft workshop lighting, realistic aluminum sheen, stainless fasteners, and workbench textures, no logos, no readable labels, and no identifiable faces. Keep the kit centered and uncluttered so it reads cleanly as a frame component set.
